zh.lifeSummary: 當Jonathan Rose Sr.於1679年9月30日出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州哈特福德維瑟斯費爾德時，他的父親Daniel Rose48歲，母親Elizabeth Goodrich33歲。他於1706年2月26日與Abigail Hale在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州哈特福德維瑟斯費爾德結婚。他們至少是10個兒子和5個女兒的父母。他去世於1767年10月8日在美國麻薩諸塞州哈普登格蘭維爾享年88歲，埋葬在美國麻薩諸塞州哈普登格蘭維爾格蘭維爾 特麥 洛阿德 其特利。

nameMeanings.name: Rose
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, French, Walloon, Danish, and German: from the name of the flower, Middle English, Old French, Middle High German rose (from Latin rosa), in various applications. In part, it is a topographic name for someone who lived at a place where wild roses grew, or a topographic or habitational name referring to a house bearing the sign of the rose. It is also found, especially in Europe, as a nickname for a man with a ‘rosy’ complexion (compare 4 below). In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates and similar-sounding names from other languages, e.g. Hungarian Rózsa (see Rozsa ), Slovak Róža and Czech Roza . Compare 6 below and French Larose 2.
English: from the Middle English female personal name Rohese, Roese, later Rose, Royse (ancient Germanic Hrodohaidis, Rothaid, composed of the elements hrōd ‘fame, renown’ + haid(is) ‘kind, sort’).
English and Scottish: variant of   Ross  .

variants.name: Rosenbach
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Hans.
German: habitational name from any of numerous places so named (see   Rose   and   Bach  ).

variants.name: Roser
meanings: German: topographic name for someone who lived at a place where wild roses grew (see Rose 1), with the suffix -er denoting an inhabitant, or an occupational name for a rose grower, or a nickname for a lover of roses.
German (Röser): habitational name for someone from any of the places called Rös, Roes, or Rösa in Bavaria, Rhineland, and Saxony.
German (Röser): variant of Rösser (see Rosser 3).

variants.name: Roseman
meanings: English (Middlesex): from the Middle English female personal name Rosamunde (ancient Germanic Rosmund, from hros ‘horse’ + mund ‘protection’). The personal name was widely understood in the Middle Ages as Latin rosa munda ‘pure rose’ and therefore as an epithet of the Virgin Mary.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): artificial name or name adopted by the husband of a woman bearing the Yiddish personal name Royze (see Rose 9).
Americanized form of German Rosemann or Rosmann (see Rossmann ).

variants.name: Roos
meanings: Dutch (also De Roos): topographic or habitational name referring to a house distinguished by the sign of a rose, or a metonymic occupational name for someone who grew roses, from roos ‘rose’.
Dutch: from the female personal name Rosa (from Latin rosa ‘rose’).
Dutch: nickname from roos ‘erysipelas’, an infection which causes reddening of the skin and scalp, applied presumably to someone with a ruddy complexion.

variants.name: Reisen
meanings: Jewish (Ashkenazic): possibly an artificial name from northeastern Yiddish reyzn ‘roses’ (standard Yiddish royzn); see Rose 9.
German: unexplained.

variants.name: Rosado
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Luis, Juan, Carlos, Rafael, Angel, Manuel, Francisco, Ramon, Miguel, Jorge, Pedro.
Spanish: nickname for someone with a notably pink and white complexion, from Spanish rosado ‘pink’, Late Latin rosatus, a derivative of rosa (see Rose ).

variants.name: Roesel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erwin, Otto.
German (Rösel): diminutive of Rose .

variants.name: Rosette
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Carlos, Jose, Arturo, Cirilo, Consuelo, Cristina, Eloy, Eufrosina, Felipe, Jesus, Jorge, Manuel.

French: from a pet form of the female personal name   Rose   6 ‘rose’, or a habitational name from any of several places called (La) Rosette, e.g. in Yonne, denoting a place where roses grow. This surname is also found in the West Indies and Mauritius.
Americanized form of Norwegian   Roseth  .

variants.name: Rozsa
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Hungarian Sandor, Tibor, Zoltan, Zsuzsanna, Balint, Gabor, Gyula, Imre, Istvan, Katalin, Laszlo, Miklos.  German Armin, Erwin, Theodor.

Hungarian (Rózsa): from the female personal name Rózsa, from the vocabulary word rózsa ‘rose’. In some cases the surname might derive from an old male personal name of the same form. Compare Roja 2 and Rose 1.

variants.name: Roese
meanings: German (Röse):
variant of   Rose  .
possibly also a habitational name from a place called Rösa near Bitterfeld.

nameMeanings.name: Jonathan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, meaning ‘God has given’, composed of the same elements as those of   Matthew  , but in reverse order. This is the name of several characters in the Bible, most notably a son of King Saul, who was a devoted friend and supporter of the young David, even when David and Saul were themselves at loggerheads (1 Samuel 31; 2 Samuel 1:19–26). The name is often taken as symbolic of steadfast friendship and loyalty. Well-known bearers of the name include the Irish clergyman and writer  Jonathan Swift  (  1667-1745 ), British theatre director  Jonathan Miller  (  b. 1934 ), British actor  Jonathan Pryce  (  b. 1947 ), and British television presenter  Jonathan Ross  (  b. 1960 ).

variants.name: Johnathan
meanings: Respelling of   Jonathan  , as if a combination of   John   and   Nathan .

en.lifeSummary: When Jonathan Rose Sr. was born on 30 September 1679, in Wethersfield, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America, his father, Daniel Rose, was 48 and his mother, Elizabeth Goodrich, was 33. He married Abigail Hale on 26 February 1706, in Wethersfield, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 10 sons and 5 daughters. He died on 8 October 1767, in Granville, Hampden, Massachusetts, United States, at the age of 88, and was buried in Main Road Cemetery, Granville Center, Granville, Hampden, Massachusetts, United States.

ja.lifeSummary: Jonathan Rose Sr.が1679年9月30日にイギリス植民地アメリカコネチカット州ハートフォードウエスエルスフィールドで生まれた時，父Daniel Roseは48歳，母Elizabeth Goodrichは33歳であった。1706年2月26日にイギリス植民地アメリカコネチカット州ハートフォードウエスエルスフィールドでAbigail Haleと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも10人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼は1767年10月8日にアメリカ合衆国マサチューセッツ州ハンプデングランヴィルで88歳で死去し，アメリカ合衆国マサチューセッツ州ハンプデングランヴィルグランヴィル センターメイン ロアド セメテリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Jonathan Rose Sr.(이)가 1679년 9월 30일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 하트퍼드 w더스필드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Daniel Rose(은)는 48세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth Goodrich(은)는 33세였다. 그는 1706년 2월 26일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 하트퍼드 w더스필드에서 Abigail Hale(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 열 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1767년 10월 8일 미국 매사추세츠 주 함p든 그랜빌에서 88세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 매사추세츠 주 함p든 그랜빌 그랜빌 세터 맹 로드 세멧리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Jonathan Rose Sr. nasceu em 30 de setembro de 1679, em Wethersfield, Hartford, Conecticute, British Colonial America, seu pai, Daniel Rose, tinha 48 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Goodrich, tinha 33 anos. Ele casou-se com Abigail Hale em 26 de fevereiro de 1706, em Wethersfield, Hartford, Conecticute, British Colonial America. Eles tiveram pelo menos 10 filhos e 5 filhas. Ele faleceu em 8 de outubro de 1767, em Granville, Hampden, Massachusetts, Estados Unidos da América, com 88 anos, e foi sepultado em Main Road Cemetery, Granville Center, Granville, Hampden, Massachusetts, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jonathan Rose Sr. родился 30 сентября 1679 г. в местности ветирсфиелд, хартфорд, Коннектикут, бритиш колоняль амирика, его отцу по имени Daniel Rose было 48 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Goodrich, 33. Он женился на женщине по имени Abigail Hale 26 февраля 1706 г. в местности ветирсфиелд, хартфорд, Коннектикут, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них было по крайней мере 10 сыновей и 5 дочерей. Он умер 8 октября 1767 г. в местности гранвилл, хампден, Массачусетс, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 88 лет и был похоронен в местности меин роад кеметири, гранвилл кентир, гранвилл, хампден, Массачусетс, Соединенные штаты.
